meta.title: MBS Question Bank — User 6 — Fresher / 1st-Year / Intern
meta.delivery: Simulation-FIRST. A day-in-the-job runs through in-tray, team chat, a tough conversation, a decision under ambiguity, an ethics moment. Self-report only for wellness.

bias_controls.telemetry: Per-item response latency, long-string & answer-change logging on every block.
bias_controls.attention_checks: Embedded attention-check + infrequency items; careless responders flagged.
bias_controls.validity_band: Each report carries a confidence band (high / interpret-with-caution).
bias_controls.scoring: Forced-choice scored with Thurstonian-IRT; behavioural/game scores weighted above self-report; methods fused by confidence, not naive mean.
bias_controls.evidence_weighting: Sim performance + internship evidence dominate the composite.

adaptive_rules.role_routing: Resume/role tags + interests fire the relevant domain sims, ICT (T) and knowledge (K) games.
adaptive_rules.path_branch: Outputs an employee-readiness path AND an intrapreneur/entrepreneur signal.
adaptive_rules.difficulty: Per-module easy/standard/stretch; aptitude shortened if UG data imported.
adaptive_rules.ai_scoring: Inbox, messages, negotiation, reflection scored by Claude.

items.stem: Age (number/slider — branches to school/college/workforce flow)
Gender
Current education level (determines subject list in Phase 11)
Internships you have done (free text + file upload — AI categorises into domain clusters)
items.constructs_fed: DEMO-EXP: Work Experience (w=1.0) | CAR-EXP: Career Exposure (w=0.8)

blocks.items_note: Game/simulation module — items generated/rendered at runtime by the engine.
Game/simulation module — items generated/rendered at runtime by the engine.

items.stem: You are managing investors' money. A risky choice could make you a big bonus but expose them to loss. Best action: (a) Take the risk — bigger reward justifies it; (b) Disclose the trade-off and let investors decide; (c) Avoid risk altogether to protect your bonus structure; (d) Take a smaller related position quietly.
A regulator informally signals everyone in your industry is moving to Standard X. You would: (a) Adopt X to fit in; (b) Wait and see; (c) Refuse unless required by law; (d) Help shape what X means.
items.constructs_fed: INST: Institutional Theory case (w=1.0) | ISO: Isomorphism: coercive/mimetic/normative (w=1.0)

items.stem: A new policy is profitable but will hurt one community group. You would most likely: (a) Prioritise shareholder return only; (b) Map all stakeholders before deciding; (c) Drop the policy; (d) Run a small pilot and adjust.
items.constructs_fed: STAKE: Stakeholder Theory orientation (w=1.0) | OB-022: Ethical attitude (w=0.4)

items.stem: Identify your company's strongest competitive advantage from these four: (a) A patented technology only you own; (b) Lowest-cost producer in the market; (c) Best customer service in your city; (d) Most charismatic CEO.
items.constructs_fed: RBV: Resource-Based View judgement (w=1.0) | VRIN: VRIN test (Valuable/Rare/Inimitable/Non-substitutable) (w=1.0)

items.stem: Your firm relies on ONE supplier for 70% of inputs. The best strategic move is: (a) Negotiate a tighter contract; (b) Build a second supplier in parallel; (c) Vertically integrate (buy the supplier); (d) Stay dependent — the supplier is reliable.
items.constructs_fed: RDT: Resource Dependence Theory (w=1.0) | ROT: Resource Orchestration (w=0.6)

items.stem: Your start-up has 5 founders, 0 process and 12 customers. The right next move is: (a) Hire a CFO and CHRO immediately; (b) Build minimum formal process for sales and product; (c) Stay informal forever — process kills innovation; (d) Sell the company while it's small.
I tailor how I explain something based on who I'm talking to.
items.constructs_fed: COMM-AUD: Audience Adaptation (w=1.0) | SS-COMM: Communication (w=0.8) | EQ-OA: Other-Awareness (w=0.5)

items.stem: In a tough conversation, I stay curious rather than defensive.
When giving critical feedback, I lead with what's working before what isn't.
I notice non-verbal cues (body language, tone) at least as much as words.
items.constructs_fed: COMM-NV: Non-Verbal Reading (w=1.0) | SOC-PERC: Social Perceptiveness (w=0.8)

items.stem: I ask follow-up questions that show I was actually listening.
I rehearse important conversations before having them.
When someone gives me feedback, I summarise it back before reacting.
I'm comfortable speaking up in groups even when I'll be the dissenting voice.
In writing, I revise multiple times until the message is sharp.
I adjust my tone in writing depending on whether the reader knows me.
I can name the specific emotion I'm feeling, not just 'good' or 'bad'.
I notice the physical sensations of my emotions (tight chest, clenched jaw).
I know what triggers my strong emotional reactions.
I can stay productive even when I'm in a difficult emotional state.
I take time to name what I'm feeling before acting on it.
I can sit with uncomfortable emotions without numbing or avoiding them.
I can sense when someone is upset even before they speak.
I can take another person's perspective even when I disagree.
In group decisions, I voice disagreement even when most people agree.
items.constructs_fed: TEAM-DA: Devil's Advocate (w=1.0) | OB-PS: Psychological Safety (w=0.4)

items.stem: I notice when a group is converging too quickly without exploring alternatives.
In teams, I sometimes do less than my share when others are doing well.
I'd rather make a decision the group can commit to than the optimal one.
I've gone along with a group decision I privately disagreed with.
I encourage quiet team members to share their views.
items.constructs_fed: TEAM-INC: Inclusive Facilitation (w=1.0) | OB-PS (w=0.6) | L-MED: Mediation (w=0.4)

items.stem: I structure team meetings so everyone speaks before we decide.
I'd rather have a vocal majority decide than negotiate consensus.
When I disagree, I push to win the argument.
I'd rather get my way than have everyone happy.
I avoid conflict whenever I can.
I'd rather change the subject than have a hard conversation.
I give up my position to keep the peace.
Others' needs come before mine in disagreements.
I split the difference to resolve disagreements quickly.
A workable middle-ground is usually fine even if neither side is fully happy.
I'm easily annoyed by small things.
I find that most people can't really be trusted.
I hold grudges longer than I should.
I often feel contempt for people who are weaker than me.
items.constructs_fed: NEG-CON: Contempt (w=1.0) | DT-P: Psychopathy (w=0.5) | SDO: Social Dominance (w=0.4)

items.stem: I replay negative events in my mind for hours afterward.
I feel suspicious of compliments — they usually have a hidden motive.
I have a short fuse when things don't go my way.
I expect the worst from new situations.
When making a choice, I keep looking until I find the BEST option, even if it takes long.
I usually settle for an option that's 'good enough' rather than the perfect one.
I often regret decisions soon after making them.
I avoid making decisions when I don't have all the information.
I'm comfortable making decisions with incomplete information.
I'd rather take a sure ₹5,000 than a 50% chance at ₹20,000.
items.constructs_fed: DEC-RA: Risk Aversion (w=1.0) | I-BIAS-LOSS: Loss Aversion (w=0.6)

items.stem: I make important decisions faster than most people I know.
I make a written pros-and-cons list for big decisions.

blocks.items_note: Game/simulation module — items generated/rendered at runtime by the engine.
Game/simulation module — items generated/rendered at runtime by the engine.

items.stem: I notice when my current learning approach isn't working and change it.
I deliberately practice the parts of a skill I'm worst at.
I can apply something I learned in one context to a totally different situation.
items.constructs_fed: LRN-TRA: Transfer of Learning (w=1.0) | I-SYN: Synthesis (w=0.6)

items.stem: I get curious about topics I don't immediately need to know.
I see myself as someone who learns for life, not just for school/work.
items.constructs_fed: LRN-ID: Learner Identity (w=1.0) | LEARN-ORI: Learning Orientation (w=0.8)

items.stem: I track what I'm learning, not just what I'm achieving.
I seek feedback even when I don't have to.
I find a way to teach what I've learned to someone else.

Your friend has misunderstood something you said. You would most likely: (a) Wait for the issue to blow over; (b) Send a brief text explaining; (c) Meet face-to-face and listen first; (d) Get a mutual friend to mediate.
items.constructs_fed: SS-COMM: Communication competence (w=1.0) | SS-COLLAB: Relationship repair (w=0.7) | EQ-EM: Empathy (w=0.5)

items.stem: Your group project has one team-mate not contributing. You would most likely: (a) Cover their part silently; (b) Tell the teacher / manager; (c) Have a direct, kind conversation with them; (d) Confront them in front of the group.
items.constructs_fed: SS-COLLAB: Collaboration & conflict skill (w=1.0) | SS-COMM: Assertive communication (w=0.7) | L-TRANS: Coaching behaviour (w=0.3)

items.stem: I plan my day and stick to my plan most of the time.
items.constructs_fed: SS-SELF: Self-Management framework (w=1.0) | SELF-GS: Goal-Setting (w=1.0) | BIG5-C: Conscientiousness (w=0.7)

items.stem: I delay things I should be doing.
items.constructs_fed: SS-SELF: Self-Management framework (w=1.0) | SELF-SC: Self-Control (w=1.0) | BIG5-C: Conscientiousness (w=0.6)

items.stem: I can break a big, unclear problem into smaller workable parts.
items.constructs_fed: SS-PS: Problem-solving (w=1.0) | STR-ANL: Analytical intelligence (w=0.6) | KATZ-CON: Conceptual skill (w=0.6)

items.stem: Rank how you prefer to absorb new information (1=most preferred, 4=least):  (a) Reading  (b) Watching / listening  (c) Discussing with others  (d) Trying it hands-on
items.constructs_fed: LR01: Learning style profile (w=1.0) | GMI-LING: Linguistic — (a) | GMI-BOD: Kinesthetic — (d)

items.stem: A teammate keeps interrupting you in a meeting. What do you do? (a) Stop talking and let them lead (b) Politely say 'let me finish my point' (c) Speak louder to be heard (d) Send a passive-aggressive message after
items.constructs_fed: SS-COMM: Communication (w=1.0) | EQ-SR: Self-Regulation (w=0.6) | ASRT: Assertiveness (w=0.8)

items.stem: Your manager gives you feedback you disagree with. You: (a) Argue your case immediately (b) Thank them, ask 2 clarifying questions, then share your view (c) Stay silent and brood (d) Complain to a peer later

blocks.items_note: Game/simulation module — items generated/rendered at runtime by the engine.

items.stem: I notice unmet needs in everyday life and think about how to solve them.
I'm comfortable taking on financial risk for an idea I believe in.
I'd rather build something new than improve something that already works.
I act on opportunities before competitors do.
I find ways to do more with less.
I can tolerate not knowing if an idea will work for months at a time.
items.constructs_fed: ENT-AMB: Ambiguity Tolerance (w=1.0) | HOF-UA: Uncertainty Avoidance (w=0.5)

items.stem: I work hard to achieve more than what is required.
items.constructs_fed: WST-ACH: Achievement/Effort (w=1.0) | MCC-N-ACH: Need for Achievement (w=0.6) | GRIT-PE: Perseverance (w=0.4)

items.stem: I set high standards for myself.
I keep going on tasks despite obstacles.
items.constructs_fed: WST-PER: Persistence (w=1.0) | GRIT-PE: Perseverance (w=0.8) | PSYCAP-R: Resilience (w=0.4)

items.stem: I rarely give up when things get hard.
I take on new responsibilities without being asked.
I willingly take charge of group projects.
items.constructs_fed: WST-LEAD: Leadership (w=1.0) | L-INI: Leadership Initiation (w=0.8) | BIG5-E: Assertiveness (w=0.4)

items.stem: I am pleasant to work with even under stress.
items.constructs_fed: WST-COOP: Cooperation (w=1.0) | BIG5-A: Agreeableness (w=0.6) | EQ-SR: Self-Regulation (w=0.4)

items.stem: I am sensitive to others' needs and feelings.
items.constructs_fed: WST-CFO: Concern for Others (w=1.0) | EQ-EM: Empathy (w=0.8) | BIG5-A: Altruism (w=0.6)

items.stem: Using my skills well and feeling accomplished is essential.
I value seeing the results of my hard work.
Career progress and challenging goals motivate me.
items.constructs_fed: OWV-ACH: Achievement Value (w=1.0) | MCC-N-ACH: Need for Achievement (w=0.6)

items.stem: I want a job where I can make my own decisions.
items.constructs_fed: OWV-IND: Independence Value (w=1.0) | SDT-AUT: Autonomy (w=0.8) | WST-IND: Independence (w=0.5)

items.stem: I want to choose how and when I work.
I dislike being told exactly how to do every task.
I prefer to work indoors most of the time.
I'm comfortable with physical labour and standing all day.
I prefer to sit at a desk most of the day.
I'm comfortable with noisy or chaotic environments.
I prefer quiet, focused work settings.
items.constructs_fed: WC-QUIET: Quiet Work (w=1.0) | BIG5-N: Sensory sensitivity (w=0.4)

items.stem: I'm willing to travel frequently for work.
If 3 pencils cost ₹24, how much do 8 pencils cost? (₹64)
I learn new languages or word patterns easily.
items.constructs_fed: GMI-LING: MI: Linguistic (w=1.0) | WAIS-VCI: Verbal Comprehension (w=1.0) | CHC-GC: Crystallised intelligence (Gc) (w=0.8) | THUR-V: Thurstone Verbal (w=1.0)

items.stem: Complete the analogy: Hand is to Glove as Foot is to ___. (Correct = Shoe / Sock)
items.constructs_fed: WAIS-VCI: Verbal Comprehension (w=1.0) | CHC-GC: Crystallised intelligence (Gc) (w=1.0)

items.stem: Vocabulary MCQ: Which word is closest in meaning to "Tenacious"?  (a) Flexible  (b) Persistent  (c) Careless  (d) Generous  [Correct = b]
items.constructs_fed: WAIS-VCI: Verbal Comprehension (w=1.0) | CHC-GC: Crystallised intelligence (w=1.0) | THUR-V: Verbal reasoning (w=1.0)

items.stem: Odd-one-out: Which word does NOT belong?  (a) Oak  (b) Pine  (c) Rose  (d) Maple  [Correct = c: Rose — not a tree]
items.constructs_fed: WAIS-VCI: Verbal Comprehension (w=0.8) | CHC-GC: Crystallised intelligence (w=0.8) | CHC-GF: Fluid reasoning (w=0.6)

items.stem: Shape analogy: Circle → Sphere; Square → ___  [Correct = Cube]
items.constructs_fed: WAIS-VCI: Verbal Comprehension (w=0.8) | CHC-GC: Crystallised intelligence (w=0.8) | STR-ANL: Analytical intelligence (w=0.6)

items.stem: Verbal analogy: Doctor : Hospital :: Teacher : ___  [Correct = School]
items.constructs_fed: WAIS-VCI: Verbal Comprehension (w=1.0) | CHC-GC: Crystallised intelligence (w=0.8) | THUR-V: Verbal reasoning (w=1.0)

items.stem: I can picture how shapes will look when rotated or rearranged.
items.constructs_fed: GMI-SPA: MI: Spatial (w=1.0) | WAIS-PRI: Perceptual Reasoning (w=1.0) | THUR-S: Thurstone Spatial (w=1.0)

items.stem: A bat and a ball cost ₹110 in total. The bat costs ₹100 more than the ball. How much does the ball cost? (Correct = ₹5)
items.constructs_fed: CRT: Cognitive Reflection (w=1.0) | WAIS-PRI: Perceptual Reasoning (w=0.4)

items.stem: If 5 machines make 5 widgets in 5 minutes, how long would 100 machines take to make 100 widgets? (Correct = 5 minutes)
items.constructs_fed: CRT: Cognitive Reflection (w=1.0) | WAIS-PRI: Perceptual Reasoning (w=0.5)

items.stem: Syllogism: All Blops are Frims. Some Frims are Goos. Therefore:  (a) All Blops are Goos  (b) Some Blops may be Goos  (c) No Blops are Goos  (d) All Goos are Blops  [Correct = b]
items.constructs_fed: CHC-GF: Fluid reasoning (w=1.0) | STR-ANL: Analytical intelligence (w=1.0) | CRT: Cognitive Reflection (w=0.6)

items.stem: Number series: What comes next?  3, 6, 12, 24, ___  [Correct = 48]
items.constructs_fed: CHC-GF: Fluid reasoning (w=1.0) | WAIS-PRI: Perceptual Reasoning (w=0.8) | RAVEN: Raven matrices style (w=0.8)

items.stem: Arithmetic: A class of 30 splits into groups of 4. How many complete groups?  [Correct = 7]
items.constructs_fed: CHC-GF: Fluid reasoning (w=0.8) | WAIS-PRI: Perceptual Reasoning (w=0.8) | THUR-N: Thurstone Number (w=1.0)

items.stem: Over the past two weeks, I have felt calm, hopeful and emotionally steady.
I have at least one person I can talk to honestly when things are hard.
items.constructs_fed: W-SOC: Social wellness (w=1.0) | SDT-REL: Relatedness (w=0.5) | ATT-S: Attachment Secure (w=0.4)

items.stem: I get enough sleep, move my body, and eat in a way that supports my energy.
I am learning new things that genuinely interest me right now.
items.constructs_fed: W-INT: Intellectual wellness (w=1.0) | MIND-G: Growth Mindset (w=0.4) | LR-CUR: Curiosity-driven learning (w=0.6)

items.stem: I feel in control of my money and not anxious about my immediate financial situation.
items.constructs_fed: W-FIN: Financial wellness (w=1.0) | MASLOW-1: Safety needs (w=0.4)

items.stem: I have a clear sense of purpose or values that guides my choices.
items.constructs_fed: W-SPI: Spiritual wellness (w=1.0) | MASLOW-5: Self-Actualisation (w=0.5) | GMI-EX: Existential intelligence (w=0.5)
